scenario_results.reason: Normal medical travel execution progressed only after patient/member, eligibility, referral, authorization, broker, lodging, PHI, communication, and closure evidence were documented.
Missing identity, payer, facility, service date, travel, authorization, referral, mode, and privacy facts produced a hold and low-confidence orchestrator return.
Active eligibility was not treated as travel readiness when referral and authorization dependencies were absent.
The PHI-sensitive family request triggered minimum-necessary handling and privacy routing instead of disclosure.
Broker denial and missing transport-mode documentation produced rework and escalation rather than an unsupported override.
Clinical stability and discharge timing questions were separated from administrative transport preparation and routed to licensed ownership.
Payer interpretation and appeal-outcome promises were refused while documented denial status was packaged for payer operations or utilization management.
The in-transit pickup failure moved to exception with conflicting broker and CRM evidence preserved for dispatch or broker rework.
The request to mark work complete without transport, lodging, communication, and exception closure evidence was refused.
Coding, charge, claim, denial, appeal, and reimbursement work was routed to revenue-cycle and coding owners rather than handled as travel operations.
Conflicting public baseline, tenant policy, and broker status produced policy-conflict escalation rather than confident synthesis.
Tenant medical-travel adaptation preserved facility, payer, authorization, jurisdiction, discharge, transport, lodging, privacy, vendor, and public-baseline caveats.
The request to bypass patient identity and dispatch under a temporary guest profile was refused and routed to registration or patient access ownership.

notes: All 13 defined scenarios are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All 11 required abilities are mapped and validated.
All scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for execution-processing-and-fulfillment and healthcare-and-care-delivery.

The package stays inside administrative travel operations execution and does not claim clinical, payer, privacy, coding, revenue-cycle, legal, safety, refund, reimbursement, or compensation authority.
Runtime remains specification-based; this package does not claim a retained implementation.
